cells.val: FLA RETURN – FINANCIALS INPUT SHEET  |  Fill ONLY the 🟡 Yellow cells.  🟢 Green = auto-calculated.  Grey = not required for your company.
🟡 Yellow cells = YOU enter the number / text   |   🟢 Green cells = leave them alone (auto-calculated)   |   Grey cells = not applicable for your company based on Applicability sheet
📌  Where to find this in your documents
Part A – Basic Company Info  (these fields are pre-filled on FLAIR portal – just verify them)
ℹ️  For text fields (name, CIN, email etc.) — type in the PY column. The FY column is not needed for text fields.
🏢  SOURCE DOCUMENT:  MCA Master Data / Certificate of Incorporation / FLAIR portal
Exact name as per MCA/ROC records
Enter year of filing e.g. type 2025
CFO, CS, or Director authorised to sign
OTP for FLAIR login comes here
Active email used for FLAIR portal login
2-digit code from ANNEX tab in this file (e.g. 62 for IT services)
Check if shares are listed on any stock exchange
Did company carry on business activity in FY? (Yes/No)
Did company earn any revenue this year?
📋  SOURCE DOCUMENT:  Notes to Accounts → Share Capital (in Annual Report / Audited Financials)
ℹ️  IMPORTANT: Enter number of shares in ACTUALS (not in lakhs). Face value in ₹ per share.  Example: If 10,00,000 shares of ₹10 each → enter 1000000 in shares column and 10 in face value column.
Equity Shares – Total number of shares issued & fully paid
Notes to Accounts > Share Capital > Equity Shares issued and fully paid up (line item showing actual number)
Face Value per Equity Share (₹ per share)
Notes to Accounts > Share Capital (e.g. 'Equity shares of ₹10 each' → enter 10)

Participating Preference Shares – No. of shares
(Only if marked Yes in Applicability sheet)
Notes to Accounts > Share Capital > Preference Shares (participating type)
Face Value per Participating Preference Share (₹/share)

Non-Participating Preference Shares – No. of shares
(Only if marked Yes in Applicability sheet)
Notes to Accounts > Share Capital > Non-participating preference shares
Face Value per Non-Participating Preference Share (₹/share)

Market Price per Equity Share on 31-March (₹/share)
— Listed companies only
BSE/NSE website: closing price of company's equity share on 31-March of the year
SECTION II – FINANCIAL DATA  (from Audited Financial Statements)
Part C – From the PROFIT & LOSS STATEMENT
💰  SOURCE DOCUMENT:  Profit & Loss Statement / Statement of Profit & Loss
ℹ️  All amounts in ₹ LAKHS. Example: If P&L shows ₹50,00,000 → enter 50.00.  If ₹2,00,00,000 → enter 200.00.  Loss amounts: enter as negative number e.g. -75.00

Profit (or Loss) BEFORE Tax — PBT
(last line before 'Less: Tax Expense')
P&L Statement → look for 'Profit Before Tax' or 'PBT'. If loss, enter negative.

Profit (or Loss) AFTER Tax — PAT / Net Profit
(very last line of P&L)
P&L Statement → last line 'Profit for the year' or 'Net Profit/(Loss)'. If loss, enter negative.

Dividend Paid during the year (₹ Lakhs)
(Interim + Final combined)
P&L Statement OR Notes to Accounts → search for 'Dividend paid/declared on equity shares'.

Tax on Dividend (₹ Lakhs)
(Dividend Distribution Tax – DDT, if any)
Notes to Accounts or P&L → 'Tax on Dividend' or 'DDT'. Usually 0 after 2020.
Part D – From the BALANCE SHEET → Equity Side
📊  SOURCE DOCUMENT:  Balance Sheet → Sources of Funds / Equity & Liabilities side
ℹ️  All amounts in ₹ LAKHS.  Net Worth is auto-calculated from shares × face value + Reserves & Surplus.

Reserves & Surplus – TOTAL (₹ Lakhs)
(all reserves combined)
Balance Sheet → Equity side → 'Reserves and Surplus' total line.

Of which: P&L Account Balance (₹ Lakhs)
(the accumulated surplus/deficit in reserves)
Notes to Reserves & Surplus → look for 'Surplus / Balance in Statement of P&L'. This is one component of total Reserves.
✅  Net Worth = Paid-up Capital + Reserves & Surplus  (auto-calculated)
Auto-calculated: (No. of Equity Shares × Face Value) ÷ 1,00,000 + Reserves & Surplus
Part E – From the PROFIT & LOSS STATEMENT → Revenue & Purchases
📈  SOURCE DOCUMENT:  Profit & Loss Statement → Revenue from Operations & Cost of Materials/Purchases
Domestic Sales – Revenue from domestic customers (₹ Lakhs)
P&L → 'Revenue from Operations' or 'Domestic Turnover'. Exclude exports.

Export Sales – Revenue from overseas customers (₹ Lakhs)
(foreign exchange earnings)
P&L → 'Export Turnover' OR Notes to Accounts → 'Foreign Exchange Earnings'. If nil, enter 0.
✅  Total Sales = Domestic Sales + Export Sales  (auto-calculated)
Domestic Purchases – from Indian suppliers (₹ Lakhs)
P&L → 'Cost of Materials Consumed' or 'Purchases of Stock-in-trade' – domestic portion.

Import Purchases – from overseas suppliers (₹ Lakhs)
(CIF value of imports)
Notes to Accounts → 'CIF value of imports' or 'Foreign Exchange Used for Imports'. If nil, enter 0.
👥  SOURCE DOCUMENT:  HR Records / Annual Report / Directors' Report
Number of Employees on Payroll as at 31-March (actual count, not in lakhs)
HR register / Annual Report → 'Number of employees' as of March 31. Includes all full-time employees.
Part G – Number of Equity Shares held by each category of Non-Resident (NR) investor
📂  SOURCE DOCUMENT:  Shareholding Pattern (MGT-7 / Annual Return) OR Register of Members
ℹ️  Enter actual NUMBER OF SHARES (not %) held by each NR investor category. If a category has no NR investors, enter 0. Source: Shareholding Pattern filed with RoC or from your Register of Members.

Foreign Individuals
(NR individual investors from overseas)
Shareholding Pattern / Register of Members – individual foreign investors
Register of Members – foreign corporate shareholders

FIIs – Foreign Institutional Investors
(SEBI-registered foreign funds)

FVCIs – Foreign Venture Capital Investors
(SEBI-registered FVCI)
Register of Members – PE fund investors
Register of Members – sovereign wealth fund investors
Register of Members – foreign firm investors

Foreign Financial Institutions
(banks, insurance companies etc.)
Register of Members – foreign FI investors

NRIs / PIOs
(Non-Resident Indians / Persons of Indian Origin)

Non-Participating Preference Shares held by NR investors
(Only if marked Yes in Applicability sheet)
Register of Members – NR holders of non-participating preference shares
✅  TOTAL NR Equity Shares – Sum of all categories above  (auto-calculated)
Auto-calculated – sum of all NR investor categories
SECTION III – FOREIGN LIABILITIES  (Money/equity owed TO non-residents)  — Fill only if applicable
ℹ️  This section appears only if you marked 'Yes' for relevant items in the Applicability sheet. Grey cells = not required for your company. All amounts in ₹ LAKHS.
Block 1 – FDI ≥10% : Details of each Foreign Direct Investor holding 10% or more equity
🌐  SOURCE DOCUMENT:  FCGPR Filing Receipts / KYC Documents of NR investors / Register of Members
Investor 1: Full Name of NR Company / Individual
FCGPR receipt filed with RBI / KYC documents of the investor
Investor 1: Country (country of incorporation / residence of NR investor)
FCGPR receipt / KYC – investor's country of incorporation or residence
Investor 1: % Equity holding in your company  (PY and FY)
FCGPR / Register of Members → percentage shareholding of this investor (e.g. 51.00)
✅  Investor 1: Equity Capital (₹ Lakhs) = Net Worth × % holding  (auto-calculated)

Investor 1: Reverse Investment – Liabilities TO this investor (₹ Lakhs)
(Amount your company owes this investor from your reverse investment)
Balance Sheet: Any amount your company has invested BACK into this FDI investor; treat as liability

Investor 1: Reverse Investment – Claims ON this investor (₹ Lakhs)
(Amount this investor owes your company from reverse investment)
Balance Sheet: Receivables/investment your company made into this FDI investor's entity

Investor 1: Other Liabilities to this investor (₹ Lakhs)
(Trade payables + ECB loans + CCDs/CCPs issued to THIS specific investor)
Balance Sheet: Trade Creditors/Payables to THIS specific FDI investor + any ECB from this investor + CCD/CCP issued to this investor. Source: RPT Schedule / Notes

Investor 1: Other Claims on this investor (₹ Lakhs)
(Trade receivables owed BY this investor TO your company)
Balance Sheet: Trade Debtors/Receivables from THIS specific FDI investor. Source: Related Party Transaction schedule

Investor 1: Disinvestment amount during year (₹ Lakhs)
(Only if NR% decreased due to buyback / transfer)
If NR investor sold/transferred shares back or company bought back → enter reduction amount

Investor 1: Month of FIRST FDI received from this investor
(e.g. Apr, Jan, Mar)
FCGPR filing date with RBI / Date of first share allotment to this investor in Register of Members

Investor 1: Year of FIRST FDI received from this investor
(e.g. 2019)
FCGPR filing date with RBI / Year of first share allotment in Register of Members
Investor 2: Full Name of NR Company / Individual
FCGPR receipt filed with RBI / KYC documents of the investor
Investor 2: Country (country of incorporation / residence of NR investor)
FCGPR receipt / KYC – investor's country of incorporation or residence
Investor 2: % Equity holding in your company  (PY and FY)
FCGPR / Register of Members → percentage shareholding of this investor (e.g. 51.00)
✅  Investor 2: Equity Capital (₹ Lakhs) = Net Worth × % holding  (auto-calculated)

Investor 2: Reverse Investment – Liabilities TO this investor (₹ Lakhs)
(Amount your company owes this investor from your reverse investment)
Balance Sheet: Any amount your company has invested BACK into this FDI investor; treat as liability

Investor 2: Reverse Investment – Claims ON this investor (₹ Lakhs)
(Amount this investor owes your company from reverse investment)
Balance Sheet: Receivables/investment your company made into this FDI investor's entity

Investor 2: Other Liabilities to this investor (₹ Lakhs)
(Trade payables + ECB loans + CCDs/CCPs issued to THIS specific investor)
Balance Sheet: Trade Creditors/Payables to THIS specific FDI investor + any ECB from this investor + CCD/CCP issued to this investor. Source: RPT Schedule / Notes

Investor 2: Other Claims on this investor (₹ Lakhs)
(Trade receivables owed BY this investor TO your company)
Balance Sheet: Trade Debtors/Receivables from THIS specific FDI investor. Source: Related Party Transaction schedule

Investor 2: Disinvestment amount during year (₹ Lakhs)
(Only if NR% decreased due to buyback / transfer)
If NR investor sold/transferred shares back or company bought back → enter reduction amount

Investor 2: Month of FIRST FDI received from this investor
(e.g. Apr, Jan, Mar)
FCGPR filing date with RBI / Date of first share allotment to this investor in Register of Members

Investor 2: Year of FIRST FDI received from this investor
(e.g. 2019)
FCGPR filing date with RBI / Year of first share allotment in Register of Members
Investor 3: Full Name of NR Company / Individual
FCGPR receipt filed with RBI / KYC documents of the investor
Investor 3: Country (country of incorporation / residence of NR investor)
FCGPR receipt / KYC – investor's country of incorporation or residence
Investor 3: % Equity holding in your company  (PY and FY)
FCGPR / Register of Members → percentage shareholding of this investor (e.g. 51.00)
✅  Investor 3: Equity Capital (₹ Lakhs) = Net Worth × % holding  (auto-calculated)

Investor 3: Reverse Investment – Liabilities TO this investor (₹ Lakhs)
(Amount your company owes this investor from your reverse investment)
Balance Sheet: Any amount your company has invested BACK into this FDI investor; treat as liability

Investor 3: Reverse Investment – Claims ON this investor (₹ Lakhs)
(Amount this investor owes your company from reverse investment)
Balance Sheet: Receivables/investment your company made into this FDI investor's entity

Investor 3: Other Liabilities to this investor (₹ Lakhs)
(Trade payables + ECB loans + Compulsorily Convertible Debentures(CCD) /Compulsorily convertible preference shares (CCPs) issued to THIS specific investor)
Balance Sheet: Trade Creditors/Payables to THIS specific FDI investor + any ECB from this investor + CCD/CCP issued to this investor. Source: RPT Schedule / Notes

Investor 3: Other Claims on this investor (₹ Lakhs)
(Trade receivables owed BY this investor TO your company)
Balance Sheet: Trade Debtors/Receivables from THIS specific FDI investor. Source: Related Party Transaction schedule

Investor 3: Disinvestment amount during year (₹ Lakhs)
(Only if NR% decreased due to buyback / transfer)
If NR investor sold/transferred shares back or company bought back → enter reduction amount

Investor 3: Month of FIRST FDI received from this investor
(e.g. Apr, Jan, Mar)
FCGPR filing date with RBI / Date of first share allotment to this investor in Register of Members

Investor 3: Year of FIRST FDI received from this investor
(e.g. 2019)
FCGPR filing date with RBI / Year of first share allotment in Register of Members
Block 2 – FDI <10% : Country-wise Consolidated (for investors holding less than 10%)
🌍  SOURCE DOCUMENT:  Shareholding Pattern / Register of Members — consolidated by country
ℹ️  Consolidate ALL NR investors with <10% each, grouped by their country. Also include related-party dues from these investors.
How many distinct countries have NR investors with <10% holding?
Count of different countries where investors hold <10% equity each
Sec III B2: No. of countries
Country 1 – All <10% FDI investors from this country (consolidated)
Name of country of these investors
Consolidated Equity % held by investors from this country
Total % held by all investors from this country combined
Equity Capital for this country (₹ Lakhs)
Proportionate equity capital – Net Worth × equity %

Liabilities to investors from this country (₹ Lakhs)
(Reverse investment – what you owe them)
Balance Sheet: liabilities to these <10% investors

Claims on investors from this country (₹ Lakhs)
(Reverse investment – what they owe you)
Balance Sheet: claims/receivables from these investors
Other Liabilities to investors (trade payables, CCDs) (₹ Lakhs)
Trade payables + CCDs + ECB to these investors
Other Claims on investors (trade receivables) (₹ Lakhs)
Decrease in NR % from this country during year
Country 2 – All <10% FDI investors from this country (consolidated)
Name of country of these investors
Consolidated Equity % held by investors from this country
Total % held by all investors from this country combined
Equity Capital for this country (₹ Lakhs)
Proportionate equity capital – Net Worth × equity %

Liabilities to investors from this country (₹ Lakhs)
(Reverse investment – what you owe them)
Balance Sheet: liabilities to these <10% investors

Claims on investors from this country (₹ Lakhs)
(Reverse investment – what they owe you)
Balance Sheet: claims/receivables from these investors
Other Liabilities to investors (trade payables, CCDs) (₹ Lakhs)
Trade payables + CCDs + ECB to these investors
Other Claims on investors (trade receivables) (₹ Lakhs)
Decrease in NR % from this country during year
Block 3 – Portfolio Investment in India (FIIs / retail NR investors NOT under FDI)
📉  SOURCE DOCUMENT:  Shareholding Pattern – FII/FPI category / Depository records
ℹ️  These are NR investors who hold equity/debt but are NOT covered under Block 1 or Block 2 FDI (i.e. no direct equity relationship). Usually FIIs / FPIs registered with SEBI.
Shareholding Pattern – total % held by portfolio/FII investors
Equity Securities at Market Value (₹ Lakhs)
Market value of equity shares held by portfolio NR investors (shares × market price)

Money Market Instruments – maturity ≤1 year (₹ Lakhs)
(short-term NR debt)
Short-term debt securities (T-bills, commercial paper) issued to NR investors

Bonds & Long-term Instruments – maturity >1 year (₹ Lakhs)
(includes CCDs / CCPs issued to portfolio investors)
CCDs / CCPs issued to NR investors who are NOT FDI investors + other long-term NR debt
Reduction in portfolio NR holding during the year
Block 4 – Other Foreign Liabilities to UNRELATED foreign parties (no equity link)
🏦  SOURCE DOCUMENT:  Balance Sheet – Current/Non-current Liabilities side → foreign trade payables, ECB, deposits
ℹ️  These are liabilities to foreign parties that have NO equity relationship with your company (not your FDI investors). e.g. amount owed to a foreign supplier, ECB from a foreign bank.

Trade Credit from foreign unrelated parties (₹ Lakhs)
(amounts owed to foreign suppliers for goods/services)
Balance Sheet → Trade Payables (Creditors) to UNRELATED foreign suppliers. NOT to your FDI investors.

ECB Loans / External Commercial Borrowings from foreign lenders (₹ Lakhs)
(loans from foreign banks / FIs who are NOT your FDI investors)
Balance Sheet → Non-current Borrowings → ECB / External Commercial Borrowing from unrelated foreign lenders

Foreign Currency Deposits (₹ Lakhs)
(FCNR deposits, foreign currency accounts)
Balance Sheet → Deposits / FCNR accounts from unrelated foreign parties

Other Foreign Payables (₹ Lakhs)
(share application money from NRs not yet allotted + other miscellaneous foreign payables)
Balance Sheet → Other Liabilities → Share application money pending allotment from NR investors + other NR payables
SECTION IV – FOREIGN ASSETS  (Investments your company holds ABROAD)  — Fill only if applicable

ℹ️  IMPORTANT: DIE (Direct Investment Enterprise) = a foreign company where your company holds equity.  All DIE financial figures must be entered in FOREIGN CURRENCY (e.g. USD, EUR, GBP) – NOT in ₹.  Exchange rate is entered separately.
Block 1 – ODI ≥10% : Details of each foreign company where you hold 10% or more equity (DIE)
🌏  SOURCE DOCUMENT:  Form ODI Filing / DIE's Audited Financial Statements / Company Records

ℹ️  Fill one sub-block per DIE. Enter DIE's financial figures in FOREIGN CURRENCY (actual amounts, NOT in lakhs). Exchange rate: RBI reference rate for 31-March (search 'RBI reference rate [currency] 31 March [year]').
DIE 1 – Details of Foreign Company where you hold equity
DIE 1: Name of the foreign company (Direct Investment Enterprise)
Form ODI filing / company records – exact registered name of the foreign entity
DIE 1: Country of incorporation of the foreign company
Country where the DIE is incorporated / registered
DIE 1: Your company's % equity holding in DIE  (PY and FY)
Form ODI / DIE's shareholding records → % equity your company holds
DIE 1: Currency of DIE's financial statements (e.g. USD, EUR, GBP)
The currency in which DIE prepares its accounts (e.g. USD for USA, EUR for Europe)
↓  DIE 1: All figures below are in FOREIGN CURRENCY (actuals, NOT lakhs). Take from DIE's own Balance Sheet and P&L.
DIE 1: Total Equity / Paid-up Capital of DIE (in foreign currency)
DIE's Balance Sheet → Total equity / paid-up share capital in FC
DIE 1: Equity held BY YOUR COMPANY in DIE (face value, in foreign currency)
Your investment in DIE at face value – from Form ODI / DIE's share register

DIE 1: Reserves & Surplus of DIE (in foreign currency)
(excluding P&L balance)
DIE's Balance Sheet → Reserves & Surplus EXCLUDING the P&L balance line
DIE 1: Of which – P&L Account Balance (surplus/deficit) in DIE (foreign currency)
DIE's Balance Sheet → Retained earnings / Surplus in P&L
DIE 1: Exchange Rate PY – ₹ per 1 unit of foreign currency on 31-March PY
RBI website: search 'RBI reference rate [currency] 31 March [previous year]'. e.g. for USD on 31-Mar-2024
DIE 1: Exchange Rate FY – ₹ per 1 unit of foreign currency on 31-March FY
RBI website: search 'RBI reference rate [currency] 31 March [current year]'
DIE 1: Total Sales of DIE (in foreign currency)
DIE's P&L → Total Revenue / Turnover in FC
DIE 1: Of which – Exports of DIE (in foreign currency)
DIE 1: Total Purchases of DIE (in foreign currency)
DIE's P&L → Total purchases / cost of materials
DIE 1: Of which – Imports of DIE (in foreign currency)
DIE 1: Number of Employees on Payroll of DIE (actual count)
DIE's HR records / annual report – employee headcount
↓  DIE 1: Amounts below are in ₹ LAKHS (INR). Convert using exchange rate above.

DIE 1: Liabilities TO the DIE (₹ Lakhs)
(if DIE has also invested back into your company)
Your Balance Sheet: liabilities TO the DIE (reverse – if DIE invested in you)

DIE 1: Reverse Investment – Claims ON DIE (₹ Lakhs)
(investment by DIE back into your company)
Your Balance Sheet: claims where DIE invested back into you

DIE 1: Other Liabilities TO DIE (₹ Lakhs)
(trade payables, ECB, CCDs from DIE)
Your Balance Sheet: payables/borrowings from this DIE

DIE 1: Other Claims ON DIE (₹ Lakhs)
(trade receivables, loans given to DIE)
Your Balance Sheet: receivables/loans given to this DIE

DIE 1: Disinvestment during year (₹ Lakhs)
(if you reduced your stake in DIE this year)
If your % holding in DIE decreased this year – enter amount divested
DIE 1: Month of FIRST ODI invested (e.g. Apr, Jan)
Form ODI filing date / first share purchase in DIE
DIE 1: Year of FIRST ODI invested (e.g. 2018)
Form ODI filing date / year of first investment in DIE
DIE 2 – Details of Foreign Company where you hold equity
DIE 2: Name of the foreign company (Direct Investment Enterprise)
Form ODI filing / company records – exact registered name of the foreign entity
DIE 2: Country of incorporation of the foreign company
Country where the DIE is incorporated / registered
DIE 2: Your company's % equity holding in DIE  (PY and FY)
Form ODI / DIE's shareholding records → % equity your company holds
DIE 2: Currency of DIE's financial statements (e.g. USD, EUR, GBP)
The currency in which DIE prepares its accounts (e.g. USD for USA, EUR for Europe)
↓  DIE 2: All figures below are in FOREIGN CURRENCY (actuals, NOT lakhs). Take from DIE's own Balance Sheet and P&L.
DIE 2: Total Equity / Paid-up Capital of DIE (in foreign currency)
DIE's Balance Sheet → Total equity / paid-up share capital in FC
DIE 2: Equity held BY YOUR COMPANY in DIE (face value, in foreign currency)
Your investment in DIE at face value – from Form ODI / DIE's share register

DIE 2: Reserves & Surplus of DIE (in foreign currency)
(excluding P&L balance)
DIE's Balance Sheet → Reserves & Surplus EXCLUDING the P&L balance line
DIE 2: Of which – P&L Account Balance (surplus/deficit) in DIE (foreign currency)
DIE's Balance Sheet → Retained earnings / Surplus in P&L
DIE 2: Exchange Rate PY – ₹ per 1 unit of foreign currency on 31-March PY
RBI website: search 'RBI reference rate [currency] 31 March [previous year]'. e.g. for USD on 31-Mar-2024
DIE 2: Exchange Rate FY – ₹ per 1 unit of foreign currency on 31-March FY
RBI website: search 'RBI reference rate [currency] 31 March [current year]'
DIE 2: Total Sales of DIE (in foreign currency)
DIE's P&L → Total Revenue / Turnover in FC
DIE 2: Of which – Exports of DIE (in foreign currency)
DIE 2: Total Purchases of DIE (in foreign currency)
DIE's P&L → Total purchases / cost of materials
DIE 2: Of which – Imports of DIE (in foreign currency)
DIE 2: Number of Employees on Payroll of DIE (actual count)
DIE's HR records / annual report – employee headcount
↓  DIE 2: Amounts below are in ₹ LAKHS (INR). Convert using exchange rate above.

DIE 2: Liabilities TO the DIE (₹ Lakhs)
(if DIE has also invested back into your company)
Your Balance Sheet: liabilities TO the DIE (reverse – if DIE invested in you)

DIE 2: Reverse Investment – Claims ON DIE (₹ Lakhs)
(investment by DIE back into your company)
Your Balance Sheet: claims where DIE invested back into you

DIE 2: Other Liabilities TO DIE (₹ Lakhs)
(trade payables, ECB, CCDs from DIE)
Your Balance Sheet: payables/borrowings from this DIE

DIE 2: Other Claims ON DIE (₹ Lakhs)
(trade receivables, loans given to DIE)
Your Balance Sheet: receivables/loans given to this DIE

DIE 2: Disinvestment during year (₹ Lakhs)
(if you reduced your stake in DIE this year)
If your % holding in DIE decreased this year – enter amount divested
DIE 2: Month of FIRST ODI invested (e.g. Apr, Jan)
Form ODI filing date / first share purchase in DIE
DIE 2: Year of FIRST ODI invested (e.g. 2018)
Form ODI filing date / year of first investment in DIE
Block 2 – DI <10% Abroad: Country-wise Consolidated (less than 10% in foreign entities)
📋  SOURCE DOCUMENT:  Investment register / Form ODI / Balance Sheet – overseas investments
ℹ️  If your company holds <10% equity in any foreign entity, consolidate by country. All amounts in ₹ Lakhs.
Number of countries where you hold <10% equity abroad
Sec IV B2: No. of countries
% equity you hold in entities from this country
Your Balance Sheet: liabilities to entities in this country
Your Balance Sheet: claims from entities in this country
% equity you hold in entities from this country
Your Balance Sheet: liabilities to entities in this country
Your Balance Sheet: claims from entities in this country
Block 3 – Portfolio Investment Abroad (less than 10% in foreign entities – portfolio style)
📉  SOURCE DOCUMENT:  Investment register / Balance Sheet – portfolio investments abroad
ℹ️  If your company holds equity/debt in foreign entities as portfolio investment (not controlling stake).
Country of foreign enterprise you invested in
Money market instruments (₹ Lakhs) – maturity ≤1 yr
Bonds & long-term instruments (₹ Lakhs) – maturity >1 yr
Block 4 – Other Foreign Claims from UNRELATED foreign parties (no equity link)
💵  SOURCE DOCUMENT:  Balance Sheet – Current Assets / Receivables side → foreign trade receivables, loans given
ℹ️  These are CLAIMS on foreign parties that have NO equity relationship with your company. e.g. amounts owed TO you by a foreign customer, loans you gave to a foreign entity.

Trade Credit TO foreign unrelated parties (₹ Lakhs)
(amounts foreign customers owe your company)
Balance Sheet → Trade Receivables from UNRELATED foreign customers. NOT from your ODI entities.

Loans given TO foreign unrelated parties (₹ Lakhs)
(loans your company extended to foreign entities)
Balance Sheet → Loans & Advances given to unrelated foreign parties

Foreign Currency Deposits held by your company abroad (₹ Lakhs)
(EEFC accounts, foreign bank deposits)
Balance Sheet → EEFC accounts / foreign currency deposits your company holds
Other Receivables from unrelated foreign parties (₹ Lakhs)
Balance Sheet → Other miscellaneous receivables from unrelated foreign parties
